name: TuSimple
tagline: Autonomous trucking company that collapsed amid espionage scandal and pivoted to AI gaming
description: TuSimple, founded in 2015 by Mo Chen and Xiaodi Hou, was an autonomous trucking company that went public in April 2021 at an $8.5B valuation, raising over $1.8B including its IPO. The company imploded after CEO Hou was fired in October 2022 for allegedly sharing confidential technology with Hydron, a Chinese hydrogen-truck startup linked to co-founder Chen. Facing FBI, SEC, and CFIUS investigations for suspected economic espionage, TuSimple wound down US operations, laid off 75% of staff, and voluntarily delisted from Nasdaq in January 2024. The company rebranded as CreateAI and pivoted to AI gaming in December 2024.
story: TuSimple went public in April 2021 at an $8.5 billion valuation, promising to put autonomous trucks on American highways. Founded in 2015 by Mo Chen and Xiaodi Hou, the company had raised $1.8 billion and built a 1,400-person operation spanning San Diego, Tucson, and Shanghai. Then it all unraveled. In October 2022, the board fired CEO Hou for allegedly funneling confidential self-driving technology to Hydron, a Chinese hydrogen-truck startup linked to co-founder Chen. The FBI, SEC, and the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States all launched investigations into suspected economic espionage. TuSimple's stock cratered. The company laid off 75% of its workforce, wound down all US operations, and voluntarily delisted from Nasdaq in January 2024. In a bizarre final act, the remnants of TuSimple rebranded as CreateAI in December 2024 and pivoted to — of all things — AI-powered video game development. What began as one of the most promising autonomous trucking ventures in the world ended as a cautionary tale about corporate governance, geopolitical risk, and the paper-thin line between technology transfer and espionage.

sources.title: TuSimple is leaving the Nasdaq as it exits the US
TuSimple pivot from self-driving to AI animation is complete with CreateAI rebrand
TuSimple U.S. Exit Clouded by Autonomous Secrets Scandal

review_flags: Funding of $1.8B+ includes IPO proceeds (~$1B). Previous description said '$800M privately' which was inconsistent with total. Corrected description to clarify. Exact pre-IPO private funding breakdown not fully verified.
